title: Open Question: (even hole, K4, diamond)-free treewidth/cliquewidth
statement_text: Is it true that (even hole, $K_4$, diamond)-free graphs have bounded treewidth (or cliquewidth)?
context_text: The paper proves bounded treewidth for (theta, triangle, $S_{i,j,k}$)-free and (even hole, pyramid, $K_t$, $S_{i,j,k}$)-free graphs. The analogous question for graphs excluding the diamond ($K_4$ minus one edge) instead of a subdivided claw is explicitly listed as open.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed; stated in the dedicated 'Open questions' section of the paper.
paper_title: (Theta, triangle)-free and (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs. Part 2 : bounds on treewidth

title: Open Question: (theta, triangle)-free graphs of bounded degree
statement_text: For every fixed integer $t \geq 4$, do (theta, triangle)-free graphs of maximum degree $t$ have bounded treewidth?
context_text: Treewidth of (theta, triangle)-free graphs is unbounded in general. For $t = 1, 2$ bounded treewidth is trivial; for $t = 3$ it follows from Corollary 4.3 in [2] (even-hole-free graphs with maximum degree $t$). The question for $t \geq 4$ is explicitly stated as open.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed; stated in the 'Open questions' section.
paper_title: (Theta, triangle)-free and (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs. Part 2 : bounds on treewidth

title: Open Question: (triangle, S1,2,3)-free graphs cliquewidth
context_text: Bounded cliquewidth is known for (triangle, $S_{1,2,2}$)-free graphs and for (triangle, $H$)-free graphs when $H$ is contained in a graph from $H_B = \{P_6, S_{1,1,3}\}$. The status of $S_{1,2,3}$ is explicitly open; the authors refer to [16] for further open problems of the same flavour.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed; stated in the 'Open questions' section.
paper_title: (Theta, triangle)-free and (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs. Part 2 : bounds on treewidth

title: Maximum Independent Set complexity for (even hole, K4)-free and (theta, triangle)-free graphs
statement_text: Determine the computational complexity of the Maximum Independent Set problem for (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs and for (theta, triangle)-free graphs.
context_text: Maximum Independent Set is polynomial for (even hole, triangle)-free and (even hole, pyramid)-free graphs. The paper gives polynomial algorithms for (theta, triangle, $S_{i,j,k}$)-free and (even hole, pyramid, $K_t$, $S_{i,j,k}$)-free graphs via bounded treewidth, but the complexity for the larger classes (even hole, $K_4$)-free and (theta, triangle)-free is stated as unknown.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed; stated in the 'Algorithmic consequences' section.
paper_title: (Theta, triangle)-free and (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs. Part 2 : bounds on treewidth

title: Maximum Independent Set complexity for H-free graphs when H contains P7, S1,1,3, or S1,2,2
statement_text: Determine the computational complexity of the Maximum Independent Set problem for $H$-free graphs whenever $H$ is some $S_{i,j,k}$ that contains either $P_7$, $S_{1,1,3}$, or $S_{1,2,2}$.
context_text: The problem is polynomial for $H \subseteq P_k$ with $k \leq 6$ and for $H \subseteq S_{i,j,k}$ with $(i,j,k) \leq (1,1,2)$, and is NP-hard when $H$ is not an induced subgraph of any $S_{i,j,k}$. The boundary region where $H$ contains $P_7$, $S_{1,1,3}$, or $S_{1,2,2}$ is listed as completely open.
notes: PDF source — math notation reconstructed; stated in the 'Algorithmic consequences' section.
